title: Sour Crunchy Turkish Tamarind Ball
description: This meal-prep dessert features a tangy tamarind ball that combines perfectly with the crunch of nuts and seeds. Perfect for those who want to enjoy a gluten-free, sour and crunchy dish as part of their breakfast or snack.
instructions: Combine tamarind paste, apple cider vinegar, and coconut sugar in a saucepan. Heat on medium-high until the mixture starts to simmer.
Remove from heat and stir in almonds, pistachios, coconut flakes, ground pistachio paste or powder, sesame seeds, and paprika until everything is well combined and evenly coated.
Transfer the mixture to an ice cream maker if you have one. Otherwise, transfer it into a sealable container with a lid for refrigeration.
Freeze the mixture in your ice cream maker according to its instructions (usually 15-20 minutes). Alternatively, let it chill in the fridge until it reaches a desired consistency (around 30 minutes).
Once frozen or chilled, use a spoon or ice cream scoop to break off small balls.
Serve immediately as is, garnished with additional toppings like chopped nuts, seeds, or honey if preferred.
